title: Chapter 19 - Reusing Styles with CSS Variables
description: Quick concept check for reusing styles with css variables.

questions.prompt: How do you define a CSS variable?

explanations.overview: Variables are declared with names that start with two dashes.

byOptionId.a: Correct. Variables are declared with names that start with two dashes.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. Variables are declared with names that start with two dashes.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. Variables are declared with names that start with two dashes.
byOptionId.d: Not quite. Variables are declared with names that start with two dashes.

questions.prompt: How do you use a CSS variable in a property value?

explanations.overview: Use the var() function to read a variable value.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. Use the var() function to read a variable value.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. Use the var() function to read a variable value.
byOptionId.c: Correct. Use the var() function to read a variable value.
byOptionId.d: Not quite. Use the var() function to read a variable value.
